      <NavItem label="Delete user" href="/users/manage" />
      <NavItem label="Remove member" href="/users/manage" />
    </Sidebar>
  );

Canonical label set I’d use:

- Primary destructive action: `Delete`
- Object-specific labels: `Delete user`, `Delete team`, `Delete seat`
- Avoid `Remove` unless the action only detaches something without deleting it.
- Avoid `Archive` unless the record remains recoverable and visible in an archived state.

How the detectors differ on this fixture:

- `action_label_drift` is the narrow, UI-action-specific detector. It sees destructive action aliases in component labels and flags that the same action is being presented with different verbs.
- `copy_ia_drift` is the broader copy / information-architecture detector. In this fixture it reports the same evidence because the inconsistent action labels also create IA ambiguity: agents and reviewers cannot tell whether Delete, Remove, and Archive are separate flows or one flow with inconsistent copy.
